enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts all meta-information lines as a set (ignoring order), and all data records remain identical

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the set of key=value pairs from each structured meta-line, ignoring their order within the angle brackets

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of GT, Number=A, Number=R, and Number=G fields
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping GT indices and Number=A/R/G field values accordingly, the parsed variant semantics must be identical: all allele indices in GT must be consistently remapped (0=REF unchanged), Number=A values must follow the new ALT order, Number=R values must keep REF first then follow new ALT order, and Number=G values must follow the genotype ordering based on the new allele ordering. The canonical representation of the variant set is unchanged.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section <ref> (see Genotype Ordering).

A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation invariance of query methods on htsjdk parsed records
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method: getNAlleles (same total allele count), isBiallelic (unchanged), isVariant (unchanged), isIndel (unchanged), isSNP (unchanged) — since the variant semantics are preserved under ALT permutation with consistent remapping.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section <ref> (see Genotype Ordering).

enforced.oracle: The canonical normalized representation (parsimonious, left-anchored) of the variant must be identical between the original and transformed records. Parsing both forms and normalizing must yield the same REF, ALT, and POS.

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String. For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field)

enforced.oracle: The set of variant alleles and their per-sample genotypes must be equivalent between the multi-ALT record and the split single-ALT records. Number=A INFO/FORMAT arrays must be correctly partitioned, and GT indices must be remapped to preserve REF (0) semantics.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: left_align_indel normalization invariance in homopolymer runs
enforced.oracle: The left-shifted indel must represent the same biological variant as the original. Parsing both forms and normalizing (left-aligning, trimming common affixes) must yield identical REF, ALT, and POS.

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.mr_name: write roundtrip normalization invariance via htsjdk serializer
enforced.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and parsing the re-serialized VCF must produce identical canonical representations (same REF, ALT, POS, QUAL, FILTER, INFO, FORMAT, GT values) for every record. Any difference exposes a writer bug in the primary SUT.

evidence.quote: ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles. These alleles do not have to be called in any of the samples.
REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.mr_name: fileformat first line violation causes rejection
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit the record) when ##fileformat is not the first line of the file; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality violation causes rejection
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit the record) when an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count that does not equal the number of ALT alleles; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

enforced.mr_name: missing required fixed column causes rejection
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit the record) when a data line is missing one of the 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO); silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: We state explicitly that zero length strings are not allowed, this includes the CHROM and ID column, INFO IDs, FILTER IDs and FORMAT IDs.

enforced.mr_name: getStart and getEnd query invariance under meta-line shuffle
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method. getStart() returns the 1-based POS, getEnd() returns the 1-based END (or POS+len(REF)-1), getLengthOnReference() returns the span on the reference. Shuffling meta-lines is a pure ordering change that must not affect coordinate-derived query results.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: POS invariance under sample column permutation
enforced.oracle: POS values for all records remain unchanged after permuting sample columns, because POS is a fixed field that describes the reference position independent of sample ordering.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1. Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM.

enforced.oracle: parse(x) == parse(sut_write_roundtrip(shuffle_meta_lines(x))); reordering meta lines (except ##fileformat) must not change parsed content

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
[VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation round-trip invariance with GT remapping
enforced.oracle: parse(x) == parse(sut_write_roundtrip(permuted(x))); reordering ALT alleles with corresponding GT index remapping and Number=A/R field reordering must produce semantically identical variant representation

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Information field format] * A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

[VCF v4.5 — Information field format] * R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-information line shuffle preserves query method results
preconditions: has at least 2 meta-information lines beyond ##fileformat
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T shuffles meta-information lines (keeping ##fileformat first). Per VCF v4.5 §Meta-information lines, meta lines other than ##fileformat may appear in any order, so a spec-compliant parser must produce identical parsed records regardless of meta-line ordering.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field reordering preserves query method results
preconditions: header has at least one structured ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER line with multiple key=value pairs
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T reorders key=value pairs inside structured meta-lines. Per VCF v4.5, implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the set of INFO key=value pairs from each record, ignoring their semicolon-delimited order

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value `.' if none are present.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs may appear in any order, but describes them as a 'series' without ordering constraints. The example DP=154;MQ=52;H2 shows no implied ordering semantics.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts all variant records and header semantics; the BCF codec re-indexes dictionary entries so the order of ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER declarations does not affect the logical content

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.

ambiguity_flags: The IDX field can override implicit dictionary positions; if IDX is present in the BCF, the numbering must match the implicit dictionary.

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation invariance with query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each query method, where P invokes the listed methods on each parsed record and compares scalar outputs; sample column order does not affect per-record variant properties

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; however, no spec rule mandates a particular sample order, and sample identity is determined by the column header, not position.

quarantine.mr_name: GT allele index out of bounds causes rejection
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit the record) when a GT allele index exceeds the number of ALT alleles; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value.
n_allele = 2 (Number of alleles, including REF)

quarantine.mr_name: POS-END interval invariance under VCF-BCF round-trip
quarantine.oracle: For each record, the interval [POS, END] (or [POS, POS+len(REF)-1] when END is absent) must be identical before and after the VCF->BCF->VCF round-trip. The BCF encoding stores POS as 0-based internally (int32_t 0-based leftmost coordinate) and converts back to 1-based on VCF output; any off-by-one error in the conversion will shift the interval.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
POS           int32_t      0-based leftmost coordinate
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

ambiguity_flags: The END field is described as a 'computed field' that 'when present, must be set' — it is not mandatory for all records, so the oracle must handle records without END by computing the interval as [POS, POS+len(REF)-1]

quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(sut_write_roundtrip(inject_equivalent_missing_values(x))); adding FORMAT fields with missing '.' values is semantically a no-op and must round-trip identically

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.

[VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] Suppose this INFO field contains the "AC=.", indicating that the AC field is missing from a record with two alt alleles. The correct representation is as the typed pair of AC followed by a MISSING vector of type 8-bit integer: 0x01.

quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(sut_write_roundtrip(permute_sample_columns(x))); sample column ordering is a presentation detail and must not affect parsed content

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, though by convention it is a presentation detail

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(sut_write_roundtrip(shuffle_info_field_kv(x))); semicolon-separated INFO key=value pairs are order-independent per VCF spec

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Fixed fields] INFO keys must match the regular expression, please note that "1000G" is allowed as a special legacy value. Duplicate keys are not allowed.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs are order-independent, though by convention they are

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffling preserves query method results
preconditions: at least one data record has multiple INFO key=value pairs (semicolon-separated)
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T shuffles the semicolon-separated key=value entries in the INFO column. The INFO field is a semicolon-delimited list of key=value pairs whose order carries no semantic meaning per the VCF spec.

evidence.quote: The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs on data lines are order-independent, but the structured meta-line field ordering rule (p121) and the fact that INFO is a semicolon-delimited dictionary imply key order is not semantically meaningful.
